name: TAO Failed Break Repair v1
thesis: TAO 4h is the structurally different lane for testing failed-break repair, but expressed as a broader reclaim-and-repair structure rather than the already-killed strict hold grammar. The edge should come from downside rejection plus fast reclaim, not from blind bottom-fishing.
invalidation_condition: Abort if failed-break repairs remain too sparse, if fast reclaim still lacks enough extension after costs, or if TAO only generates untradable volatility noise.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price breaks below short-term support, quickly reclaims value, and then extends modestly as the failed break is repaired.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: In chop, failed breakdowns should occur more often than clean continuation trends. A broader reclaim expression should participate more honestly than strict hold choreography.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: TAO 4h is a fair structurally different test lane because failed breakdowns and sharp repairs are more visible on a volatile asset.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If failed breakdowns only bounce weakly and do not extend, the reclaim will still lack enough post-cost edge.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If entries engage but the second leg is too brief, reduce target ambition before altering the reclaim condition.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: A decent number of repair trades with first-target harvesting doing most of the expectancy.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: TAO 4h plus at least one cross-lane show real repair density and enough post-cost travel after reclaim.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, low-trade mirage, or repairs that die immediately at value.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; breakdown and reclaim are sequential and broad
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed by broad reclaim logic instead of exact candle choreography

validation_basket.why_included: Most expressive lane for failed-break repair in the current basket.
validation_basket.continue_if: The pattern prints enough repairs to justify the management structure.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: TAO only produces chaos with no repaired second leg.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Sharp failed breaks, fast reclaim, then a modest repaired continuation leg.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the mechanism survives on a cleaner major rather than only a wild lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: BTC shows the same repair shape with acceptable structure.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: The idea only appears on TAO and disappears elsewhere.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Fewer but cleaner failed-break repairs than TAO.

validation_basket.why_included: Determines whether the mechanism generalizes to a cleaner similar-asset lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH still produces tradable failed-break repairs, even with lower amplitude.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: Mechanism vanishes on the cleaner lane.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Smaller but more orderly failed-break repairs.

long: Time-based de-risk after 2 bars if unrealized gain < 0.12 * ATR_14 by selling 15%
short: Time-based de-risk after 2 bars if unrealized gain < 0.12 * ATR_14 by covering 15%
